meaning: <日>n.[体](柔道中的)一胜[日文汉字:一本]
examples: He won the competition with ippon.
To win the contest a judoka must score an Ippon (a degree equalling 10 points) by using a successful technique.
Alina Alexandra Dumitru of Romania defeated Yanet Bermoy of Cuba with an ippon to win gold in Judo Women's -48kg.
Masato Uchishiba, the 2000 world student champion, clinched gold for Japan, defeating Slovakia's Jozef Krnac for knockout "ippon" finish in the under-66kg final.
Burton missed out on the semi-finals but earned a shot at a medal by beating France's Guillaume Elmont in the repechage event with an ippon score.
